meta.title: Bearing Life Calculator | ISO 281 L10 Formula | AluCalc
meta.description: Calculate the L10 rating life of rolling element bearings according to ISO 281 standards using dynamic load rating and equivalent load.

seo.intro: The L10 life is the basic rating life of a bearing. It represents the life that 90% of a group of identical bearings can be expected to achieve or exceed before the first signs of fatigue appear.

variables.C: Basic dynamic load rating (N or lbf)
variables.P: Equivalent dynamic bearing load (N or lbf)
variables.p: Life exponent (3 for ball bearings, 10/3 for roller bearings)

seo.step_by_step:
1. Determine the basic dynamic load rating (C) from the manufacturer's catalog.
2. Calculate the equivalent dynamic load (P) acting on the bearing.
3. Choose the exponent (p) based on bearing type (ball vs. roller).
4. Apply the formula to find the life in millions of revolutions.
seo.practical: Essential for maintenance scheduling, equipment design, and reliability engineering in industrial machinery.
seo.example: Scenario: A ball bearing (p=3) with C=15,000 N and P=1,500 N. L10 = (15000 / 1500)^3 = 10^3 = 1,000 million revolutions.
